steps.title: Notice the awaiting guest
steps.content: The passage presents Miss Pyne dressed and watching, Martha bringing strawberries and flowers, the June evening, and the house standing open. These details all build toward the long-expected guest's arrival.

steps.title: Reject narrow purposes
steps.content: A invents worry, B focuses on change over time that is not developed, and C overstates a contrast between indoors and outdoors. The passage mainly depicts the setting and preparations while characters await a visitor. The answer is D.
